prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
## Unclenched

The final keystroke fades to still—  
No more the gnawing, urgent will  
That drove me through the weary night,  
Chasing the stubborn, fading light.  

The cluttered desk, the coffee cold,  
The thousand doubts I couldn't hold  
Back from the edge... now hushed, released,  
A weight dissolved, a tension ceased.  

I stretch my arms to empty air,  
Breathe deep a calm beyond compare.  
No frantic pulse, no racing mind—  
Just quiet space, unbound, unlined.  

The work stands done. I stand apart,  
Hollowed out, yet full of heart.  
A strange, soft ache—a phantom limb—  
For the great labor now grown dim.
